title: Evaluating on Test Data
concept: Calculating loss and classification accuracy on unseen test data to verify generalization.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of the training loop in machine learning?

options.text: To improve the model's performance over time

exercises.explain: The training loop is designed to improve the model's performance by adjusting weights based on the calculated loss.

cards.title: Evaluating Model Performance
cards.body: When we train a model, we need to check how well it works on new, unseen data. This is called evaluation.

cards.title: Loss and Accuracy
cards.body: Loss measures how far off a model's predictions are from actual values. Classification accuracy shows how many predictions were correct.

cards.title: Like a Sports Tournament
cards.body: Just like a team checks their performance against other teams after each match, we evaluate our model using test data to see if it generalizes well.

cards.title: Example of Evaluation
cards.body: If our model predicts 7 out of 10 images correctly, we can say it has a classification accuracy of 70%.

cards.title: Myth: More Data Equals Better Accuracy
cards.body: It's a myth that just having more data will always improve accuracy. We also need good quality data and proper evaluation.

cards.title: Understanding Loss and Accuracy
cards.body: Loss shows how wrong the model is, and accuracy shows how many predictions were right.

exercises.prompt: What does a lower loss indicate in model evaluation?

options.text: The model is using more data

exercises.explain: A lower loss indicates that the model's predictions are closer to the actual values, meaning it's performing better.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Classification accuracy can be calculated only on training data.
exercises.explain: Classification accuracy should be calculated on test data to assess how well the model generalizes to unseen examples.

exercises.prompt: To verify generalization, we evaluate our model on __________ data.
exercises.sentence: To verify generalization, we evaluate our model on unseen test data.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'test', as we evaluate on data that the model hasn't seen during training.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements about evaluating a model on unseen data is correct?

options.text: It helps to check if the model is just memorizing training data.
It is unnecessary for model improvement.
It only needs to be done occasionally.

exercises.explain: Evaluating on unseen data is crucial because it ensures the model is learning to generalize, not just memorize the training data.

exercises.prompt: What are the two key metrics used to evaluate a model's performance on test data?

options.text: Number of training epochs and data size

exercises.explain: The two key metrics to evaluate a model's performance on test data are loss, which indicates how wrong the predictions are, and classification accuracy, which tells us the percentage of correct predictions.
